programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Andrea fights to save her relationship and reputation by exposing Gabriel as the Sun Hill sniper - but editor Bruce Malcolm has other ideas. Dale reprimands Colin Fairfax for his racist attitude, while Samantha gives evidence at the trial of serial rapist Alan Kennedy and manages to outmanoeuvre a defence QC who accuses her of securing a confession by means of psychological torture(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Andrea's cover has been well and truly blown, leaving her to face the wrath of Gina and fatally compromising her testimony at the trial of rapist Alan Kennedy, who is acquitted. Desperate for a chance to redeem herself, she makes one last effort to gather incriminating evidence against Gabriel - only to end up being held hostage in a store room by the crazed copper. Events come to a head when Colin is dismissed after being exposed as a vicious racist and retaliates by handcuffing Ken in a petrol-doused van and crashing it into Sun Hill's front office. Blissfully unaware of the escalating crisis, Reg calls Marilyn's mobile phone at the station, intending to ask for her hand in marriage - and triggers a massive explosion(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: An inferno sweeps through Sun Hill station in the aftermath of the explosion, giving Gabriel the chance to make good his escape, callously leaving Andrea trapped under fallen debris as the flames creep closer. Dale also faces a fiery demise in the burning building, while Jim struggles to accept the news that his friend Ken was killed instantly when the van blew up. Marilyn fights for life in hospital, a grief-stricken Reg at her bedside - and as he tearfully slips an engagement ring onto her finger, an ominous bleep from her cardiac monitor heralds a tragic end to their relationship(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kezia and Smithy work together on a courier van hijack and soon get a result, but later find themselves in trouble for scaring one of Keith Durante's rape victims. James Tennant's fiancee turns up at the station with news that he has evidence of the whereabouts of his missing daughter Amy, but it's clear that seven months of frantic searching have left him a broken man(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A forgetful clock-mender contacts the sleuth when he becomes the target of a couple of small-time crooks. Armed with advice from DCI Adams, Hetty starts her investigation at a video store and soon discovers that there is more to the case than meets the eye(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Now that Hetty and her husband are back from their trip to Australia, the Wainthropp Detective Agency can get back to some serious sleuthing. However, the first case to crop up is far from typical - it involves hunting for buried treasure in the wilds of Cumbria. Patricia Routledge stars as the modern-day Miss Marple, with Derek Benfield, Dominic Monaghan and Lesley Dunlop(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Change is on the way to the sleepy market town of Cranford as the railway pushes out from Manchester, but many of the female residents are more excited about the arrival of the handsome new doctor, Frank Harrison. Adapted by Heidi Thomas, with an all-star cast including Judi Dench and Eileen Atkins, who was awarded a Bafta for her role as the authoritative Miss Deborah Jenkyns(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Dr Harrison's plans to become better acquainted with Sophy are thwarted by Caroline Tomkinson and Clara Smith, Mr Carter takes pity on an illiterate 10-year-old and Miss Matty is shaken to see a face from her past. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in the town, but Lady Ludlow regrets any notion of seasonal charity when Mr Carter asks her to intervene in the case of Job Gregson, who has been blamed for a spate of thefts. Miss Matty is delighted when Mr Holbrook invites her to his farm, and Dr Harrison's friend Jack sends Caroline Tomkinson a card hinting she should expect a proposal from the medic. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Philip Glenister(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Dr Harrison tells Rev Hutton of his intentions toward Sophy and asks permission to court her, blissfully unaware that the generosity he has shown to Mrs Rose has accidentally created an unlikely love triangle. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods(n)

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The true nature of friendship within Cranford is revealed as the ladies rally to help Miss Matty when she receives terrible news. Meanwhile, Dr Harrison suffers as his career, reputation and relationship all seem ruined, while Miss Galindo and Mr Carter grow closer as they face up to the tragedy of Hanbury. Judi Dench, Philip Glenister and Simon Woods star(n)

programme.title: Miss Marple

Feature-length mystery, starring Joan Hickson, Donald Pleasence and Adrian Lukis(n)

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy

At the parish council meeting, chairman Howard Mollison introduces his plans to turn the community centre into a health spa, but he reckons without local solicitor Barry Fairbrother, who makes an impassioned speech about the importance of the centre to the impoverished people of the nearby estate. However, an unexpected turn of events leaves an empty seat on the council - leading to the biggest battle Pagford has ever seen. Michael Gambon, Rory Kinnear, Keeley Hawes and Julia McKenzie star(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Watford v Liverpool at Vicarage Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace at Amex Stadium. The Reds have been in fine from this season, but had to work hard for a 2-0 win in the reverse fixture, which was Nigel Pearson's first match in charge of Watford. Meanwhile the clash between rivals Brighton and Palace took on greater importance than ever, given neither side are safe from the drop. Plus, Bournemouth v Chelsea, Newcastle United v Burnley and West Ham United v Southampton(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Cybermen are on the march and as the last remaining humans are ruthlessly hunted down, Graham, Ryan and Yaz face a terrifying fight to survive. Civilisations fall and others rise anew, while lies are exposed, truths are revealed and battles are fought. And for the Doctor - trapped and alone - nothing will ever be the same again.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where items brought along include an incredibly rare Beatles guitar with an enviable history, which turns out to be one of the most valuable items ever seen on the show. Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia's relationship comes under increasing strain, while Ted's arrival from New Zealand raises questions. Raff is left speechless when a giraffe turns up at Far Slack Farm and Gillian is riddled with old anxieties when it brings unwarranted attention to the barn. Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, as all four teams looked too boost their chances of playing European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Football Stars in Bad Cars
programme.desc: Fulham team-mates Tom Cairney, Steven Sessegnon and Marcus Bettinelli drive a £2,000 hatchback for the day and play games set by BBC Radio 1's Chris Stark via the car radio. The players then pass the car on to Norwich City after giving it a makeover so that it is even more embarrassing to drive(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal

programme.desc: Keegan has high hopes for the business when Chantelle helps him with a loan application. But as he and Tiffany head home, they are stunned to bump into a burglar escaping from their flat. Gray reassures Whitney as he visits her in prison, and Suki's lies come close to unravelling during a conversation with Jean(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of thousands of people fall victim to scams in the UK every year, with many are run from criminal call centres in India, where teams of fraudsters operate around the clock. Panorama has obtained hacked CCTV footage from inside one scam call centre that shows exactly how it works, with reporter Rajini Vaidyanathan tracking down the man behind the scam and hearing from British victims who have been conned(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Secrets come out and lies unravel as Hannah deals with the fallout from her anniversary party, with Christie furious and pained by the discovery that she had an abortion in the early months of her marriage. The whole Noble Hale Defoe team attends a prestigious awards ceremony at the Law Association, where Ruth is given a lifetime achievement award. Despite enjoying the limelight, Ruth warns Hannah and Zander that she will not be stepping down any time soon. Abi Morgan's drama, starring Nicola Walker(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: An infamous Mucklowe family member returns to the village and causes Kerry to re-evaluate her living arrangements. Meanwhile, Reverend Seaton and Kurtan help Len get back on his feet while he is waiting for the council to arrange accommodation for him. Spoof documentary presenting a wry look at the lives of two ne'er-do-well cousins living in the Cotswolds, written by and starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Barry-born make-up entrepreneurs Hannah and Sophie have a photoshoot in Ibiza, while Gonth makes a new friend who shares his passion for fast cars(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jon Richardson, team captain on 8 Out of 10 Cats, hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, warming up the crowd before introducing routines by fellow comedians Sara Pascoe and Nathan Caton(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: FIFA Women's World Cup Golden Boot winner Ellen White, Great Britain 400m runner Perri Shakes-Drayton, former Everton midfielder Leon Osman and Grand National-winning jockey Mick Fitzgerald join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright joins the council housing team investigating a Liverpool street invaded by vermin, and in Lowestoft he meets a tenant living in a newly converted former takeaway(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bed semi in Bilston, a quirky end terrace with an annex in Scholar Green, Stoke-on-Trent, and a flat in Croydon(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Couple Pam and Claire head to Auckland, New Zealand, to spend a week gaining an insight into the jobs, property and lifestyle available to them if they decided to move down under(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Footage shows the moment a teenager fell from a waterfall, and on a busy dual carriageway, motorists attempt to rescue a man who has passed out at the wheel of his van(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from the Isle of Anglesey in north Wales, where experts Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall advise two competing teams anxious top make maximum profit at auction. Anita also meets up with gold prospector Vincent Thurkettle, who struck gold just off the nearby coast(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Part one of two. Abz' life is turned upside down after a night out, so Rob steps in to try and help. Zara invests in a new car(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Drama, starring Kacey Ainsworth as a blind woman who undergoes an operation to restore her sight after 20 years. Her family persuade her that she is doing the right thing, but life as a sighted person proves a devastating upheaval. Nothing looks the way she remembered it, her beloved guide dog is taken away, and she discovers her job is a position specifically created to employ a disabled person(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent, and learns more about the UK's first nature reserves(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester, where expert Duncan McKean and furniture Chloe Kempster transform an old radiogram and a chest of drawers(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the two childhood friends assisting each of them, who grew up watching the original version of the show. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts, and disrupts the chefs' best efforts with his 10-minute Challenge Wild Cards(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Liverpool (Kick-off 7.45pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the fifth-round tie at Stamford Bridge. The Blues defeated Championship sides Nottingham Forest and Hull City in the previous rounds, while Liverpool overcame rivals Everton, before beating League One Shrewsbury Town in a replay while fielding the youngest side in the club's history. The sides met here in the Premier League in September, with the Reds prevailing 2-1. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Ian Wright and Glen Johnson(n)

programme.title: Beauty Laid Bare
programme.desc: Four members of the public investigate the secrets of the beauty industry, beginning by travelling to the Hollywood Hills to attend one of the world's biggest make-up conventions. They discover how social media is becoming an increasingly important tool in the industry's marketing machine, and question the environmental impact of plastic packaging(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Journalist Nikki Fox teams up with scientists at Liverpool John Moores University to discover what takeaways do to our health, what's really in them and how people can kick the habit. In a unique experiment, 15 volunteers eat nothing but takeaways for two weeks and undergo a barrage of tests, from blood sugar and body fat to gut bacteria and mental sharpness. The results reveal the shocking truth about how takeaways affect our bodies and brains(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke attempt to transform a stagnant, weed-choked pond into a haven both for its owners and the local wildlife(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedians Jamali Maddix and Bobby Mair compete in West Yorkshire, guided by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Angus Ashworth. Between browsing for bargains and battling for the best punchline, Angus and Bobby explore the fascinating history of the printed page around the city of Leeds, while Jamali and Natasha explore how the strange evolution of the optical toy led to the profusion of games and media encountered today. In spite of advice, one funnyman thinks a porcelain parrot will be profitable, while the other invests in a toy robot. The duo then take their purchases to auction in Bourne End, Buckinghamshire(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Following gardeners in Orkney, Shetland and the Black Isle as they show how they have created stunning sites right on the very edges of Scotland in difficult conditions(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Jasper Carrott back to Barry Island in south Wales, where the comedian reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1958(n)

programme.title: Scott of the Antarctic (1948)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following explorer Robert Falcon Scott's failed expedition to be the first to reach the South Pole. The extreme conditions, poor decision-making and the pressure of the race to make it to the Pole before their Norwegian rivals proved too much for the British team, resulting in tragedy in 1912. Starring John Mills, Derek Bond, James Robertson Justice, Kenneth More and John Gregson(n)

programme.title: Cycling
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of day five at the Velodrom in Berlin. Husband and wife Jason and Laura Kenny boast 10 Olympic titles between them, and are targeting more global success at these championships. Laura is likely to complete a busy week by contesting the Madison, while Jason is vying for a third sprint world title. With eight finals being held over the weekend, plenty of other British and global stars are looking to make their mark as the championships - and Olympic qualifying period - draw to a close. With analysis from Chris Hoy, and commentary by Simon Brotherton and Chris Boardman(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores unusual and unexplained natural events that occur around the world during winter, enlisting the aid of leading scientists to uncover why these strange events happen. The programme examines unusual weather conditions, including tornadoes made of snow, and animals behaving in unexpected ways, from snowboarding crows to penguins attempting death-defying feats(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin head to Israel, where they discover four extraordinary homes, designed to combat the heat and inspired by rich cultural and historical traditions. Among the properties are Barud House in Jerusalem, made from 50,000 white stone tiles, arranged into a symmetrical pattern that casts shadows across the house all day. The final stop is the village of Musmus and the House of Three Gardens, where Tel Aviv architect Ron Fleisher has designed a house that combines traditional Islamic architecture with modernism(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A family of five works in a corner shop to reveal how such businesses have changed over the course of the past 100 years, aided by presenter Sara Cox and social historian Polly Russell. The first episode covers shops from the Victorian era to the First World War. The family must learn to bake their own counter goods, measure loose products like tea, flour and sugar by hand, and make deliveries with a horse and cart. They also receive a visit from Kelly Holmes, who puts them through an Edwardian workout and discover the impact of food shortages on corner shops as the war drew on(n)

programme.title: Russia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The traveller's second leg of his journey across Russia takes him from south-east Siberia to the far south west and the majestic Caucasus Mountains. Along the way he stops off at the oldest lake on earth, witnesses a commemoration of the Second World War in the city of Irkutsk and visits a cafe where patriotic staff and punters perform a daily rendition of the national anthem. He also finds out how the collapse of communism has seen millions of disenchanted Russians join new sects and religions, with one community worshipping a man claiming to be the reincarnation of Christ(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris put emergency vehicles to the test to see if the fire brigade, the police or the ambulance service has the fastest vehicles. Chris pays tribute to rally driver Colin McRae, Paddy puts the new BMW M8 through its paces, and comedian Tom Allen joins the team in the studio(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: The two remaining couples pay a visit to the empty property without Duane and Rena, to get a sense of what their new life in the wilderness will really be like. One pair immediately starts hatching grand plans, but tensions are beginning to emerge for their competitors. Eventually, Duane and Rena arrive and set two final tasks - one couple must cut windows into the loft and the other must set up a new windsock on the airstrip. Finally, in the evening, they are each invited to state their cases over why they want to live here before a winner is announced and Duane and Rena bid farewell to their former home(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests, including Sara Pascoe(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Scottish creativity on a journey from Oban to Glasgow. Travelling through the Highlands, Frankie visits the home of Grant Morrison, who has written stories for some of the comic world's most iconic characters. Resuming his trek, he travels from Dunoon to Bannockburn, the site of Robert the Bruce's most famous battle, where he learns how to fight with a sword and shield just like the Scots Knights of the 1300s. Frankie also witnesses young artists brightening up the darker corners of Glasgow and reflects on the city's shameful past, profiting off slavery in the British colonies(n)

programme.title: Manchester by the Sea (2016)
programme.desc: An irritable loner is forced to return to the coastal town where he grew up following the sudden death of his brother. He is appointed guardian of his teenage nephew, and as he struggles to cope with his newfound responsibilities, he also finds himself confronted with a past tragedy. Drama, starring an Oscar-winning Casey Affleck, with Michelle Williams and Kyle Chandler(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce hosts the political debate from Middlesbrough, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley examines the cases of an insurance cheat who exploited the Manchester bombing, and a fantasist who defrauded the NHS for thousands of pounds(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric is determined to do anything he can to help the relative of a former patient, despite the AAU being filled to capacity and the hospital being in the midst of a nursing crisis. Essie realises that she has to decide where she stands with Sasha when Ben makes a grand gesture. Kian returns from Holby from Canada to discover that Jac has been taking a step back from her work, and resolves to force her out of her comfort zone(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max finds herself in a desolate place after receiving some distressing news, and following her rift with Ric, she may have lost her closest confidante. Meanwhile, Jac and Kian must decide who will be Darwin's next clinical lead, and shocking revelations threaten Essie and Dom's friendship. Starring Hugh Quarshie and Marcus Griffiths(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Blackburn Cathedral, Charles Hanson values a rare Eureka clock and Anita Manning is drawn to four oil paintings of the Ribble Valley. Host Paul Martin meets up with Michelin-star chef Nigel Haworth to discover how to make the perfect Lancashire hotpot(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assist the red and blue teams as they look for items of interest and value in Detling, Kent. Presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp monitors proceeding and also visits a nearby farm to lend a hand with the hop harvest(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Week three of the culinary sees Mary Berry ask the contestants to produce their ultimate Sunday brunch, after which Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish in just one hour using a sweet ingredient. They all push themselves to the limits to impress the judges, knowing that whoever serves up the least popular dishes have to compete in the eliminator round, for which chef Angela Hartnett has selected a technically complicated recipe(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Properties in St Albans, Hertfordshire, are judged by neighbours, beginning with a 1930s space-saving conversion, a Victorian terrace, and a traditional two-up, two-down(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen go on a culinary journey around the nation, following a route mapped out by the adventurous gourmand Samuel Chamberlain in his 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. They begin in the Dorset seaside town of Weymouth, where they sample lots of fresh seafood before heading to the countryside to eat Portland lamb(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Paudge is enraged when he suspects Tony Doyle has been abusing Aoife Burke, while Dunne discovers Donna Burke's murderer could be a lot closer to home than he thought(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: Architecture critic Tom Dyckhoff hosts the competition searching for untapped interior design talent, with experts Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood acting as judges. In the first heat, three hopefuls must transform a bedroom each in a modernist former leisure centre in south London and have three days, £1,000 and a small team of assistants to help deliver their schemes(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo trawls through the National Archives at Kew in south-west London to dig out stories from previously classified documents. In the first episode, he unearths the secret files of Britain's last hangman, explores private letters sent to Sherlock Holmes, and examines the hidden history of murderer Dr Crippen(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Dover Castle, Jonathan Pratt discovers an elegant Lalique figure and James Lewis finds a collection of Chinese snuff bottles. Paul Martin looks at the history of the castle(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a journey across Northern Ireland to Argyll and Bute, beginning by travelling from Newry to Portadown(n)

programme.title: Coastal Path
programme.desc: Traversing the rugged and mysterious north Cornish coast, explorer Paul Rose's first stop is Morwenstow, where he squeezes into the Trust's smallest property, the Hawker's Hut. Built in the 1800s by opium-smoking poet Reverend Hawker, it is popular with walkers who add their own graffiti. Next, Paul enjoys a tour of the wild and majestic Tintagel Castle, before heading to Padstow where the Mayday 'Obby 'Oss celebrations are in full swing. Paul is then put through his paces by a group of youngsters training to be surf lifesavers at Newquay's Fistral beach(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contenders to brave the black chair, answering questions on the specialist subjects of British breweries, the life and works of John Lennon, Lorenzo de' Medici and the Back to the Future trilogy. They then each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Orwellians take on the Turophiles in the last of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Colman's Mustard, Torino FC, Lamborghini, Merrill Lynch(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the sixth of the quarter-finals, two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: Police and specialists investigate a double stabbing in Colchester's homeless community that leaves Carl Hopkins dead and the other victim seriously injured. Detectives initially question whether the men had attacked one other, and take to the streets to search for witnesses, but people are reluctant to talk(n)

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Seven disparate characters share their stories with a camera, their fates inevitably, inextricably, and unknowingly set for a head-on collision. Starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Maxine Peake, Phil Davis, Sara Kestelman, Sandra Gayer and Ioanna Kimbook(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Eighty-one-year-old Joe has been left with a hole in his heart after suffering a heart attack, and faces a complex procedure that carries a high-mortality rate. Ellie, 28, is one of the growing population of adults with congenital heart disease, who had devices fitted into their hearts as children, and need further procedures as they get older. Already cancelled twice before, she hopes that this time her operation to replace her pulmonary valve with keyhole surgery will go ahead, so she can begin to plan her wedding to Jade(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: First-time farmers Paige and Kingsley are hoping that showing their sheep at the Isle of Wight show will bring them closer to their life dream of becoming full-time farmers(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria & Albert curators Steph and Jenny plan a major new exhibition celebrating the work of British fashion designer Mary Quant. A Yorkshire woman donates a 1960s minidress that would be perfect - but it needs a lot of restoration work to make it exhibition-ready. Curator Julius uncovers some of the first objects ever given to the museum, including a hand-painted writing case that once belonged to Queen Victoria, while Corinna hits the streets to gather the most significant designs of today - the flags and logo of climate change activists Extinction Rebellion(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which experts transform and then sell three things that were about to be dumped at a tip(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple returning from living in Sweden for 25 years who want to find a home in Wiltshire, and goes for a ride with a professional carriage driver(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the fifth round of fixtures, including Leeds Rhinos v Warrington Wolves, St Helens v Toronto Wolfpack and Hull FC v Catalans Dragons(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: A family home with an industrial twist, a sleek, split-level semi-detached property, and an eclectic Edwardian abode with a menagerie of surprises are all judged by neighbours(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: In the ancient market town of Devizes, Terry Wogan and cabbie Mason McQueen are on the hunt for the finest flavours in town. They sample local foods including the famous Wiltshire cured bacon, and navigating the famous Calne locks helps them appreciate a home-made bread and butter pudding(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Kevin Dunne is devastated to hear Tom Callaghan's account of what happened with Donna Burke. The pressure of the case, and protecting Callaghan, takes its toll(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The three remaining competitors aim to impress while working in Kentish weatherboard cottages, having to abide by restrictions on what can and cannot be changed in the Grade II listed buildings. One designer struggles with lighting fixtures and fittings, a second is accused of playing it safe and the other faces tense negotiations over the wallpaper. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff, with judges Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood deciding which two designers will make it through to the next round(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his trawl through the National Archives, unearthing Hitler's hidden medical files, examining the Christmas cards sent to an African dictator, and revealing the details of an incredible shopping list by Henry VIII(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Adam Partridge search for antiques at Rochester Cathedral in Kent. Meanwhile, Paul Martin explores the story of the French Huguenots(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a journey from Belfast to Portrush, Michael Portillo investigates the work of author and Narnia creator CS Lewis, and visits the Royal Portrush Golf Club(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris put emergency vehicles to the test to see if the fire brigade, the police or the ambulance service has the fastest vehicles. Chris pays tribute to rally driver Colin McRae, Paddy puts the new BMW M8 through its paces, and comedian Tom Allen joins the team in the studio(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns get a taste of running a store in the 1920s, 30s and 40s, considered the golden age of the corner shop due to the plentiful availability of packaged food. However, rationing quickly has a devastating impact on both the shop and the family's day-to-day lives, with carrot lollies and mutton bacon proving less than enticing meals. The Arderns learn how to make ice cream by hand and blend tea, as well as taking delivery of a vintage fruit machine. They also find themselves at the centre of a community party recreating the VE Day celebrations(n)

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: A forensic search of a property gives the police the breakthrough they need in the search for Carl Hopkins' murderer. However, it also produces evidence that complicates the case - it seems the victim may have robbed the suspect, raising the possibility that he was killed in self-defence(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam is at the Hamletville theatre festival trying out a new play for Broadway. Comedy-drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Putin: The New Tsar
programme.desc: The story of Vladimir Putin's extraordinary rise to power - from a lowly KGB colonel to Boris Yeltsin's right-hand man and ultimately his successor as Russian President. The documentary contains revelations from former confidante Sergei Pugachev who helped Putin to power, before falling from favour. Chess grandmaster Garry Kasparov recounts his failed attempt to stand against him, and oligarch Mikhail Khordokovsky - who was jailed and stripped of his wealth - speaks of the consequences of experiencing the wrath of Putin. Other contributors include veteran politicians Jack Straw and William Hague(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: As a boy, frogs were the first animals David Attenborough kept and today he is still just as passionate about them. In this film, he takes viewers into the weird and wonderful world of the amphibian, shedding new light on the charismatic, colourful and frequently bizarre creatures, and examining their anatomy, extraordinary behaviour and ability to survive in some of the most extreme places on the planet(n)

programme.title: The Science of Doctor Who
programme.desc: Brian Cox takes an audience at the Royal Institution of Great Britain's lecture hall on a journey into the universe of sci-fi series Doctor Who, which celebrated its 50th anniversary last month. Brian reveals the science behind the show and explains the physics that allows the Doctor to travel through space and time, and also looks at evidence for the existence of extraterrestrial life. Featuring an appearance by Matt Smith(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Jacky Klein and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit Belfast to take in the Ulster Museum, and assess what are thought to be low-value copies of works by Peter Breughel the Younger. They also head to the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont, where they view a controversial image. Said to depict William III and the Pope, the painting was once slashed with a knife, and is now kept in a room away from public view(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the golden age of piracy during the early 18th century, when Captain Kidd, Blackbeard, Calico Jack and others were in operation on the high seas. He charts the devastating impact they had during an era of colonial expansion and how they became the most wanted outlaws in the world by plundering the vast network of maritime trade, they became the most wanted outlaws in the world(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Diaries
programme.desc: A candid profile of the writer, following him in his home village in Yorkshire, to his community library in Primrose Hill and during a trip to New York. In a series of intimate encounters filmed over the period of a year, he reflects on his modest beginnings and his enduring gratitude to a Welfare State that paid for his education and looked after his parents in their old age(n)

programme.title: The History Boys (2006)
programme.desc: Eight unruly grammar school boys preparing to take their Oxbridge entrance exams are exposed to the contrasting teaching styles of two very different schoolmasters - the intellectually enthusiastic Hector, who seeks to instil a love of knowledge for its own sake, and the more cynical new arrival Irwin, who intends to equip them with the specific information they need to get ahead. Comedy drama adapted from Alan Bennett's play, with Richard Griffiths, Stephen Campbell Moore and Frances de la Tour(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Books
programme.desc: The return of the programme exploring classic works of fiction, this time focusing on works for younger readers. In the first of two editions, actress Samantha Bond examines E Nesbit's Five Children and It, a fantasy story about a group of siblings who discover a creature that can grant wishes. Samantha also meets authors Jacqueline Wilson and Kate Saunders, who have both published updated and re-versioned stories inspired by Nesbit's work(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Jacky Klein and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit Belfast to take in the Ulster Museum, and assess what are thought to be low-value copies of works by Peter Breughel the Younger. They also head to the Northern Ireland Assembly at Stormont, where they view a controversial image. Said to depict William III and the Pope, the painting was once slashed with a knife, and is now kept in a room away from public view(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco travels by scooter to the ancient Greek theatre at Epidaurus to experience for himself the extraordinary acoustics. Moving on to the Venetian fortress of Methoni, he learns how it became an important trading centre in the 13th century for people travelling to the Holy Land(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how the power of images has transformed the modern world, beginning with the explosion of scientific and technological advances at the start of the 20th century that created radical new ways of looking at the world(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories and fates of four people accused of blasphemy in Pakistan, including Asia Bibi, a Christian woman who claims she was falsely accused by her Muslim co-workers after a disagreement. The film also shows Khadim Hussain Rizvi's advocacy of the blasphemy law as a key platform to run for the role as prime minister of Pakistan in the forthcoming elections(n)

programme.title: Art of France
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how art in France took a dramatic turn after the Revolution ushered in a bold new world of artists including Jacques-Louis David and Theodore Gericault(n)

programme.title: Leonora Carrington: The Lost Surrealist
programme.desc: Profile of the English surrealist artist, who was a key figure in the movement's heyday in 1930s Paris, but remained a virtual unknown in the UK having moved to Mexico(n)

programme.title: Rude Britannia
programme.desc: Julian Rhind-Tutt reveals how Britain's bawdy subculture survived the restrained Victorian period. He highlights the comedy antics of booze-addled comic-strip anti-hero Ally Sloper, and lewd music hall shows by artists such as Champagne Charlie and Marie Lloyd, whose acts are re-created by contemporary performers(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer and explorer Paul Rose heads for Wensleydale as he sets out to explore the region, starting in the small town of Hawes, where locals now run many of their own services, including the buses. Paul checks out why cycling is so popular in the Dales and tries to get to the top of the Buttertubs Pass. Along the way he meets a family of dormice and spends time with a man who looks after one of the area's most imposing castles(n)

programme.title: King Alfred and the Anglo-Saxons
programme.desc: Michael Wood explores the role of Alfred the Great's grandson Aethelstan in creating a kingdom of all England. The presenter travels to Devon, Cumbria, Scotland and Rome to reveal why he believes the English monarch was one of the greatest, and tells the tale of the ruler's learning and law-making, including his development of a national coinage and his defeat of the Vikings and Scots in the Anglo-Saxon Great War(n)

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines the reign of Queen Anne, which saw England and Scotland becoming united as Great Britain and rising to become a major European power. However, Anne's skills as a world leader have long been overshadowed by the scandalous tell-all story written by rejected lady-in-waiting Sarah Churchill, which provided the basis of Oscar-winning film The Favourite. Lucy reexamines Sarah's story, and discovers it may not be entirely true(n)

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.desc: Clare Jackson examines the history of the Stuarts, who grappled with the chaos of three separate kingdoms, multiple religions and civil war during the 17th century. In the opening episode, she looks at James VI of Scotland's attempts to unite his country with England and Ireland and persuade his subjects to feel more British(n)

programme.title: Armada - 12 Days to Save England
programme.desc: Dan Snow relives the events of the naval battle in 1588, drawing on recently rediscovered documents to get a sense of the key events in the conflict and exposing the tensions among the Spanish commanders. Dramatic reconstructions go behind the scenes in Elizabeth I's royal court, revealing the impact the prospect of invasion had on the queen and her advisors as she tried to maintain a strong public image while under the threat of an assassination attempt(n)

programme.title: Puss in Boots: The Three Diablos
programme.desc: Animated short featuring the lovable cat from the hit film series Shrek. Puss in Boots helps three kittens rescue a precious ruby from the evil Whisperer. Featuring the voices of Antonio Banderas, Gilles Marini and Charlotte Newhouse(n)

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway starts a walk in the pretty seaside town of Saundersfoot in Pembrokeshire, currently under-going a multi-million pound seafront regeneration. His nine-mile trek takes him through an area rich in mining history as he heads towards Pendine Sands in neighbouring Carmarthenshire. Derek discovers some fascinating military history as the coast was used as the site for rehearsals for the D-Day landings. The walk leads on to Pendine, with its famous seven-mile stretch of beach famously used for land-speed record attempts(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Host Tudur Owen discovers the truth about later life, referencing the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of sheds, flash cars, and martial arts, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Cybermen are on the march and as the last remaining humans are ruthlessly hunted down, Graham, Ryan and Yaz face a terrifying fight to survive. Civilisations fall and others rise anew, while lies are exposed, truths are revealed and battles are fought. And for the Doctor - trapped and alone - nothing will ever be the same again.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where items brought along include an incredibly rare Beatles guitar with an enviable history, which turns out to be one of the most valuable items ever seen on the show. Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia's relationship comes under increasing strain, while Ted's arrival from New Zealand raises questions. Raff is left speechless when a giraffe turns up at Far Slack Farm and Gillian is riddled with old anxieties when it brings unwarranted attention to the barn. Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present as an audience in Llandudno questions a panel of politicians on the big issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Football Stars in Bad Cars
programme.desc: Fulham team-mates Tom Cairney, Steven Sessegnon and Marcus Bettinelli drive a £2,000 hatchback for the day and play games set by BBC Radio 1's Chris Stark via the car radio. The players then pass the car on to Norwich City after giving it a makeover so that it is even more embarrassing to drive(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a Merseyside tenant in poor health who is trapped in a damp rented property, while a Birmingham resident has vanished into thin air - leaving their home empty(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Couple Debbie and Lee Daniels head out for a trial week living in Auckland, New Zealand, to see if the move abroad would offer them the fresh start in life they are hoping for(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Stories of bravery captured on phones, CCTV and dashcams, meeting those saved as well as the rescuers whose selfless acts can be the difference between life and death. In the first edition, CCTV captures the moment two boys are rescued from a rising tide and saved by a total stranger, while a community effort saves a seal being strangled to death by a rope around its neck.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at an antiques fair in Peterborough, while Colin Young joins host Eric Knowles with advice for porcelain collectors(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel's attempt to give Jimmi a reality check does not go to plan, and Zara distracts a brooding Emma with a shocking confession. Valerie and Sid uncover an uncomfortable truth about a delivery driver's relationship(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: The return of the show created by Jimmy McGovern that features standalone dramas telling modern stories about characters who have reached turning points in their lives. A man just released from prison begins a relationship, but neglects to tell his new girlfriend he is fitted with a tag and has a 7pm curfew. Drama, starring Tom McKay and Angel Coulby(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple returning from living in Sweden for 25 years who want to find a home in Wiltshire, and goes for a ride with a professional carriage driver(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where items saved from the skip include a wooden clothes airer and a modern fire surround(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes using ingredients brought in by the contestants helping them. In the first edition, Rylan unveils the new kitchen and introduces chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid, who are joined by Falkirk couple Gemma and Brian(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Lucy Owen visits Monmouth to find out how to make homes more flood resilient. Plus, the case of a landscaper who was paid £8000 to transform a garden which he ruined(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Everything You Need to Know
programme.desc: A panel of experts and BBC reporters around the world answer questions on the coronavirus outbreak, including the latest health advice, what to do if planning a trip abroad and a look at whether there will be an impact on businesses(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keegan has high hopes for the business when Chantelle helps him with a loan application. But as he and Tiffany head home, they are stunned to bump into a burglar escaping from their flat. Gray reassures Whitney as he visits her in prison, and Suki's lies come close to unravelling during a conversation with Jean(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: In the wake of the worst flooding to hit parts of Wales in 40 years, families and communities in the South Wales valleys are struggling to get their lives back together after homes and businesses were left devastated. BBC Wales Investigates asks what price will residents have to pay to protect themselves from extreme weather events in future - and who will pick up the bill?(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Karl Lewis admits while questioned by Cadi that the accusations he brought against Ellis were false. Cadi turns her attention to Mia, whose false testimony led to Karl's arrest(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Secrets come out and lies unravel as Hannah deals with the fallout from her anniversary party, with Christie furious and pained by the discovery that she had an abortion in the early months of her marriage. The whole Noble Hale Defoe team attends a prestigious awards ceremony at the Law Association, where Ruth is given a lifetime achievement award. Despite enjoying the limelight, Ruth warns Hannah and Zander that she will not be stepping down any time soon. Abi Morgan's drama, starring Nicola Walker(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of thousands of people fall victim to scams in the UK every year, with many are run from criminal call centres in India, where teams of fraudsters operate around the clock. Panorama has obtained hacked CCTV footage from inside one scam call centre that shows exactly how it works, with reporter Rajini Vaidyanathan tracking down the man behind the scam and hearing from British victims who have been conned(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: An infamous Mucklowe family member returns to the village and causes Kerry to re-evaluate her living arrangements. Meanwhile, Reverend Seaton and Kurtan help Len get back on his feet while he is waiting for the council to arrange accommodation for him. Spoof documentary presenting a wry look at the lives of two ne'er-do-well cousins living in the Cotswolds, written by and starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jon Richardson, team captain on 8 Out of 10 Cats, hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, warming up the crowd before introducing routines by fellow comedians Sara Pascoe and Nathan Caton(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright joins the council housing team investigating a Liverpool street invaded by vermin, and in Lowestoft he meets a tenant living in a newly converted former takeaway(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bed semi in Bilston, a quirky end terrace with an annex in Scholar Green, Stoke-on-Trent, and a flat in Croydon(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Carl Jones is keen to move his family to New Zealand - despite the fact he has never been himself. His wife Jenna, however, does not share his dreams, as the furthest she has been away from home is France and moving their children to the other aside of the world seems like a step too far. The family decides to sample living in Auckland for a week to help them make a decision(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Lucy Owen visits Monmouth to find out how to make homes more flood resilient. Plus, the case of a landscaper who was paid £8000 to transform a garden which he ruined(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Footage shows the moment a teenager fell from a waterfall, and on a busy dual carriageway, motorists attempt to rescue a man who has passed out at the wheel of his van(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help two teams find items at Peterborough's East of England Showground, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp tries her hand at glass-blowing in Stourbridge(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Part one of two. Abz' life is turned upside down after a night out, so Rob steps in to try and help. Zara invests in a new car(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Drama, starring Kacey Ainsworth as a blind woman who undergoes an operation to restore her sight after 20 years. Her family persuade her that she is doing the right thing, but life as a sighted person proves a devastating upheaval. Nothing looks the way she remembered it, her beloved guide dog is taken away, and she discovers her job is a position specifically created to employ a disabled person(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent, and learns more about the UK's first nature reserves(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester, where expert Duncan McKean and furniture Chloe Kempster transform an old radiogram and a chest of drawers(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Akis Petretzikis and Anna Haugh create a meal in 20 minutes, using ingredients brought on to the show by the two childhood friends assisting each of them, who grew up watching the original version of the show. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts, and disrupts the chefs' best efforts with his 10-minute Challenge Wild Cards(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Liverpool (Kick-off 7.45pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the fifth-round tie at Stamford Bridge. The Blues defeated Championship sides Nottingham Forest and Hull City in the previous rounds, while Liverpool overcame rivals Everton, before beating League One Shrewsbury Town in a replay while fielding the youngest side in the club's history. The sides met here in the Premier League in September, with the Reds prevailing 2-1. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Ian Wright and Glen Johnson(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Barry-born make-up entrepreneurs Hannah and Sophie have a photoshoot in Ibiza, while Gonth makes a new friend who shares his passion for fast cars. In Llanelli, 17-year-old Cameron is making a mint out of creepy crawlies(n)

programme.title: Beauty Laid Bare
programme.desc: Four members of the public investigate the secrets of the beauty industry, beginning by travelling to the Hollywood Hills to attend one of the world's biggest make-up conventions. They discover how social media is becoming an increasingly important tool in the industry's marketing machine, and question the environmental impact of plastic packaging(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Journalist Nikki Fox teams up with scientists at Liverpool John Moores University to discover what takeaways do to our health, what's really in them and how people can kick the habit. In a unique experiment, 15 volunteers eat nothing but takeaways for two weeks and undergo a barrage of tests, from blood sugar and body fat to gut bacteria and mental sharpness. The results reveal the shocking truth about how takeaways affect our bodies and brains(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Watford v Liverpool at Vicarage Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace at Amex Stadium. The Reds have been in fine from this season, but had to work hard for a 2-0 win in the reverse fixture, which was Nigel Pearson's first match in charge of Watford. Meanwhile the clash between rivals Brighton and Palace took on greater importance than ever, given neither side are safe from the drop. Plus, Bournemouth v Chelsea, Newcastle United v Burnley and West Ham United v Southampton(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, with reports from around Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at the Deanery CE Academy in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sioned Young and her family embark on a trial week living in Perth, but husband Richard is concerned about the practicalities of such a huge change from their lives in North Wales(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of grandmother and granddaughters go head to head in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, under the guidance of John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Presenter Anita Manning gets 'into the groove' and has a go at record collecting(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores Kew Gardens with Alan Titchmarsh, who shares memories from presenting Songs of Praise in the 1980s and '90s and reflects on finding God in nature(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create two gardens inspired by exotic foreign locations, featuring a Moroccan one in Teignmouth and an Asian one in Bitterne(n)
