The Blues defeated Championship sides Nottingham Forest and Hull City in the previous rounds, while Liverpool overcame rivals Everton, before beating League One Shrewsbury Town in a replay while fielding the youngest side in the club's history. The sides met here in the Premier League in September, with the Reds prevailing 2-1. With analysis from Alan Shearer, Ian Wright and Glen Johnson(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Following the attack, Bob struggles with his guilt as Angus's health deteriorates. Maggie desperately tries to help Ruby cope with the baby, and Stevie takes out his frustration at Bob's attack on Poppy(n)

programme.title: Beauty Laid Bare
programme.desc: Four members of the public investigate the secrets of the beauty industry, beginning by travelling to the Hollywood Hills to attend one of the world's biggest make-up conventions. They discover how social media is becoming an increasingly important tool in the industry's marketing machine, and question the environmental impact of plastic packaging(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Watford v Liverpool at Vicarage Road and Brighton & Hove Albion v Crystal Palace at Amex Stadium. The Reds have been in fine from this season, but had to work hard for a 2-0 win in the reverse fixture, which was Nigel Pearson's first match in charge of Watford. Meanwhile the clash between rivals Brighton and Palace took on greater importance than ever, given neither side are safe from the drop. Plus, Bournemouth v Chelsea, Newcastle United v Burnley and West Ham United v Southampton(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around the Province(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at the Deanery CE Academy in Swindon(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sioned Young and her family embark on a trial week living in Perth, but husband Richard is concerned about the practicalities of such a huge change from their lives in North Wales(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of grandmother and granddaughters go head to head in the Hertfordshire town of Sawbridgeworth, under the guidance of John Cameron and Caroline Hawley. Presenter Anita Manning gets 'into the groove' and has a go at record collecting(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores Kew Gardens with Alan Titchmarsh, who shares memories from presenting Songs of Praise in the 1980s and '90s and reflects on finding God in nature(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create two gardens inspired by exotic foreign locations, featuring a Moroccan one in Teignmouth and an Asian one in Bitterne(n)

programme.title: Puss in Boots: The Three Diablos
programme.desc: Animated short featuring the lovable cat from the hit film series Shrek. Puss in Boots helps three kittens rescue a precious ruby from the evil Whisperer. Featuring the voices of Antonio Banderas, Gilles Marini and Charlotte Newhouse(n)

programme.title: Shrek 2 (2004)
programme.desc: The grouchy ogre and his new wife Princess Fiona set out on a journey to meet her parents. However, the king and queen are less than impressed with their daughter's choice of husband, while a conniving fairy godmother is determined to make sure her own son gets to marry the princess. Animated adventure sequel, with the voices of Mike Myers, Cameron Diaz, Eddie Murphy, Antonio Banderas, John Cleese, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Saunders and Rupert Everett(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits the Gwent Levels to find out more about a project celebrating the area's fishing and farming heritage, and preserving them for the future. Matt Baker meets a group of fishermen who are still catching salmon the way it's been done for centuries, Ellie Harrison is on the trail of a star species that's made an amazing comeback to the Levels, and Tom Heap is looking at what's being done to ensure those most in need in the countryside can access the healthiest fresh food(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Cybermen are on the march and as the last remaining humans are ruthlessly hunted down, Graham, Ryan and Yaz face a terrifying fight to survive. Civilisations fall and others rise anew, while lies are exposed, truths are revealed and battles are fought. And for the Doctor - trapped and alone - nothing will ever be the same again.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where items brought along include an incredibly rare Beatles guitar with an enviable history, which turns out to be one of the most valuable items ever seen on the show. Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia's relationship comes under increasing strain, while Ted's arrival from New Zealand raises questions. Raff is left speechless when a giraffe turns up at Far Slack Farm and Gillian is riddled with old anxieties when it brings unwarranted attention to the barn. Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Timothy West(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, featuring Everton v Manchester United at Goodison Park and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, as all four teams looked too boost their chances of playing European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Football Stars in Bad Cars
programme.desc: Fulham team-mates Tom Cairney, Steven Sessegnon and Marcus Bettinelli drive a £2,000 hatchback for the day and play games set by BBC Radio 1's Chris Stark via the car radio. The players then pass the car on to Norwich City after giving it a makeover so that it is even more embarrassing to drive(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets a Merseyside tenant in poor health who is trapped in a damp rented property, while a Birmingham resident has vanished into thin air - leaving their home empty(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters explore a bungalow in the village of Chelsfield and a semi-detached house in Erith, both in the south east of London. Plus, a shop with plenty of flats above(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Couple Debbie and Lee Daniels head out for a trial week living in Auckland, New Zealand, to see if the move abroad would offer them the fresh start in life they are hoping for(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Stories of bravery captured on phones, CCTV and dashcams, meeting those saved as well as the rescuers whose selfless acts can be the difference between life and death. In the first edition, CCTV captures the moment two boys are rescued from a rising tide and saved by a total stranger, while a community effort saves a seal being strangled to death by a rope around its neck.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at an antiques fair in Peterborough, while Colin Young joins host Eric Knowles with advice for porcelain collectors(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel's attempt to give Jimmi a reality check does not go to plan, and Zara distracts a brooding Emma with a shocking confession. Valerie and Sid uncover an uncomfortable truth about a delivery driver's relationship(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: The return of the show created by Jimmy McGovern that features standalone dramas telling modern stories about characters who have reached turning points in their lives. A man just released from prison begins a relationship, but neglects to tell his new girlfriend he is fitted with a tag and has a 7pm curfew. Drama, starring Tom McKay and Angel Coulby(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple returning from living in Sweden for 25 years who want to find a home in Wiltshire, and goes for a ride with a professional carriage driver(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Wallyford Recycling Centre in East Lothian, where items saved from the skip include a wooden clothes airer and a modern fire surround(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes using ingredients brought in by the contestants helping them. In the first edition, Rylan unveils the new kitchen and introduces chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid, who are joined by Falkirk couple Gemma and Brian(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Coronavirus: Everything You Need to Know
programme.desc: A panel of experts and BBC reporters around the world answer questions on the coronavirus outbreak, including the latest health advice, what to do if planning a trip abroad and a look at whether there will be an impact on businesses(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keegan has high hopes for the business when Chantelle helps him with a loan application. But as he and Tiffany head home, they are stunned to bump into a burglar escaping from their flat. Gray reassures Whitney as he visits her in prison, and Suki's lies come close to unravelling during a conversation with Jean(n)

programme.title: The Chronicles of Erne
programme.desc: In early summer, mayfly begin to hatch and fisherman Michael Shortt is fly fishing for trout. Amy Burns from RSPB NI travels to island reserves to install cameras and measure eggs(n)

programme.title: The Split
programme.desc: Secrets come out and lies unravel as Hannah deals with the fallout from her anniversary party, with Christie furious and pained by the discovery that she had an abortion in the early months of her marriage. The whole Noble Hale Defoe team attends a prestigious awards ceremony at the Law Association, where Ruth is given a lifetime achievement award. Despite enjoying the limelight, Ruth warns Hannah and Zander that she will not be stepping down any time soon. Abi Morgan's drama, starring Nicola Walker(n)

programme.title: There's No Place Like Tyrone
programme.desc: Farmer and beautician Ruth ropes in friends and family to shoot a music video for her brother Neil, a country music singer(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of thousands of people fall victim to scams in the UK every year, with many are run from criminal call centres in India, where teams of fraudsters operate around the clock. Panorama has obtained hacked CCTV footage from inside one scam call centre that shows exactly how it works, with reporter Rajini Vaidyanathan tracking down the man behind the scam and hearing from British victims who have been conned(n)

programme.title: BBC Arts NI Presents
programme.desc: NI Music Prize winners, Ryan Vail and Elma Orkestra (Eoin O'Callaghan), two of the most innovative musicians, join forces for the international multi-media project, Borders(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: An infamous Mucklowe family member returns to the village and causes Kerry to re-evaluate her living arrangements. Meanwhile, Reverend Seaton and Kurtan help Len get back on his feet while he is waiting for the council to arrange accommodation for him. Spoof documentary presenting a wry look at the lives of two ne'er-do-well cousins living in the Cotswolds, written by and starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Barry-born make-up entrepreneurs Hannah and Sophie have a photoshoot in Ibiza, while Gonth makes a new friend who shares his passion for fast cars(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jon Richardson, team captain on 8 Out of 10 Cats, hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, warming up the crowd before introducing routines by fellow comedians Sara Pascoe and Nathan Caton(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright joins the council housing team investigating a Liverpool street invaded by vermin, and in Lowestoft he meets a tenant living in a newly converted former takeaway(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a two-bed semi in Bilston, a quirky end terrace with an annex in Scholar Green, Stoke-on-Trent, and a flat in Croydon(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Couple Pam and Claire head to Auckland, New Zealand, to spend a week gaining an insight into the jobs, property and lifestyle available to them if they decided to move down under(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Footage shows the moment a teenager fell from a waterfall, and on a busy dual carriageway, motorists attempt to rescue a man who has passed out at the wheel of his van(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from the Isle of Anglesey in north Wales, where experts Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall advise two competing teams anxious top make maximum profit at auction. Anita also meets up with gold prospector Vincent Thurkettle, who struck gold just off the nearby coast(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Part one of two. Abz' life is turned upside down after a night out, so Rob steps in to try and help. Zara invests in a new car(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Drama, starring Kacey Ainsworth as a blind woman who undergoes an operation to restore her sight after 20 years. Her family persuade her that she is doing the right thing, but life as a sighted person proves a devastating upheaval. Nothing looks the way she remembered it, her beloved guide dog is taken away, and she discovers her job is a position specifically created to employ a disabled person(n)

programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent, and learns more about the UK's first nature reserves(n)

programme.desc: Journalist Nikki Fox teams up with scientists at Liverpool John Moores University to discover what takeaways do to our health, what's really in them and how people can kick the habit. programme.desc: Heather Dewar explores the Black Isle, north of Inverness. Although not an actual island, it is almost entirely surrounded by water and despite its proximity to the capital of the Highlands, feels remote and rural. Heather learns about the peninsula's greatest claim to fame - its connection with the Brahan Seer, the legendary 17th-century figure whose prophetic powers eventually led to his grisly death. Heather, a keen artist, shows that her creative skills do not extend to crafting when she attempts to help Mairi Rhind spin some yarn(n)

programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of Dominica, taking a trip into the wild rainforests to the island's most famous waterfall. He prepares twice-fried plantains on the black sands of Picard Beach, discovers a local recipe using tiny fish that gather in Dominica's rivers, and acquires some cassava bread from the Kalinago community, to serve with his Calypso chicken(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the second episode of the Battle rounds. Coaches will.i.am, Sir Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Meghan Trainor all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen from the blind auditions go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition, but those that aren't so lucky can still be stolen by another coach(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show, featuring undercover pranks, surprises galore and star guests. In this week's show, Ant and Dec are joined in the studio by Rob Brydon, who takes up the role of Star Guest Announcer, while Anne-Marie will be taking to the stage to perform in the End Of The Show Show. Plus, lucky viewers across the UK are surprised with a Place On The Plane and another audience member gets the chance to Win The Ads, and get their hands on every single item featured in the commercial breaks(n)

programme.title: Uncle Buck (1989)
programme.desc: An unreliable but good-natured slob is called upon to look after his trouble-making nephew and nieces while their parents are away - and what starts as a disaster soon changes for the better as the unlikely babysitter gets to grips with the finer points of being a responsible adult. Family comedy, starring John Candy, Macaulay Culkin, Jean Kelly, Gaby Hoffman, Amy Madigan and Laurie Metcalf(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse, tennis coach Judy Murray and actor-comedian John Thomson to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby host the semi-final of the skating contest, in which the celebrities must each perform two routines for judges Jayne Torvill, Christopher Dean, Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman in the hope of winning a place in next week's grand final. Plus, a performance by Cirque du Soleil: Crystal(n)

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort. In this episode, Harry welcomes John Barnes and Chris Kamara, where they take to the water to see what the area has to offer from the sea. Harry challenges John and Chris to a powerboat showdown on the Solent, before they sample a local delicacy at Harry's favourite chip shop(n)

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary looking at crash-for-cash scams, joy riders and brazen car thieves - with dramatic video and interviews from victims and eyewitnesses. Police officers reveal how they catch the criminals, and former car thieves expose the tactics used to take vehicles. Narrated by Jamie Theakston(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport. Staff at Heathrow airport contend with drunken passengers, drugs and drones, while Sue and Demi deal with an extra load of passengers after a closure at Gatwick airport. Elsewhere, Border Force officer Bob returns to the job, working to protect Britain's biggest border(n)

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The survivalist and adventurer continues his journey through rural Australia in search of its remarkable landscapes, extraordinary wildlife and the people who manage to survive in its wilderness. In the nation's Northern Territory in the waterways of Kakadu, Ray encounters the fearsome salt-water crocodile, the largest of which can exceed six metres in length. He also stays with an Aboriginal family whose members share the secrets of farming with fire and creating meals with ingredients procured from the Bush(n)

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: The survivalist and adventurer continues his journey through rural Australia in search of its remarkable landscapes, extraordinary wildlife and the people who manage to survive in its wilderness. In the nation's Northern Territory in the waterways of Kakadu, Ray encounters the fearsome salt-water crocodile, the largest of which can exceed six metres in length. He also stays with an Aboriginal family whose members share the secrets of farming with fire and creating meals with ingredients procured from the Bush(n)

This time, the 'love lift' delivers hypnotist Ben from London, Essex-based broker Harry, Edinburgh landscape gardener David and Paul, a 'body double' from Dublin. All four men are given a chance to win over Paddy's singletons, but to secure a date they must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Plus, an update on how last weeks' couples got on during their stay on the Isle of Fernandos(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: A selection of home videos sent in by members of the public and presented in a format to entertain people of all ages, narrated by Harry Hill(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, including footage of a golfer being surprised by an alligator, a child being fired into a belfry, and a restaurant receiving an unexpected guest through their ceiling(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring a builder dropping in through a roof, a boy getting his head trapped in a mailbox, a woman being attacked by a remote-controlled plane and a man hit by a giant trampoline during a hurricane(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: A selection of home videos sent in by members of the public and presented in a format to entertain people of all ages, narrated by Harry Hill. A wedding photographer falls into a lake, a baby ruins a mannequin challenge and a man gets his tongue caught in a mousetrap(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan spends time with Lyndsey's boyfriend in the hope of discovering why she likes him so much, while Walden encourages Jenny to take her acting career more seriously. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan persuades Walden to invite former Wonder Woman star Lynda Carter over for dinner. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Stories inspired by viewers' letters, featuring a look at the world from Stewie's point of view and a British version of the Griffins. Peter also gains the power to turn everyone in Quahog into Robin Williams by touching them(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois takes drastic action to save the life of Stewie's new best friend after his parents refuse treatment for his illness due to their religious beliefs. Meanwhile, Peter has a spiritual experience of his own. With the guest voice of Judy Greer (Archer)(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: Marcus spies a money-making opportunity when the city goes into a frenzy of fundraising at marathon time, and Jason bets Aurelius he can beat him in the big race(n)

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys launch a cabaret night in the bar when Marcus starts dating popular Roman satirist Minerva, who starts dissecting their relationship live on stage. With Aisling Bea(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Angie persuades Robert to arrange an evening out with the lads _ but neither she nor Ida are prepared for the consequences(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi receives an indecent proposal from Ray, Sarah implores Bethany to put her career before Daniel, and Tracy and Peter make sealed bids for Ken's house(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When dairy worker Debbie Moffett (played by Martine McCutcheon) is crushed to death by a giant round of cheese at the home of the world-famous Midsomer Blue, Barnaby and Jones are soon on the case. Pathologist Kate Wilding discovers the victim's daughter was being expelled from the local prep school and it emerges Debbie died just hours after an argument at a parents' council meeting. Her phone leads the detectives to Oliver Ordish, with whom she was having an affair(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: Comedian Paul O'Grady returns with another Animal Orphans, and having previously visited Africa, he now travels farther east to find out about the rehabilitation of orphaned wild animals in the Malaysian part of Borneo. In the first of two parts, he heads for the orang-utan rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, where he discovers how the animals are being threatened by forest fires, learns about the damage that overfishing is doing to sea turtles' natural habitat, and tries to persuade a gibbon to climb a tree - a skill it needs to learn before it can return to the wild(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Trans-Siberian Adventure
programme.desc: The actress embarks on her third travelogue, having previously traced the Nile from its source and sampled the varied rural, historical and urban cultures of Greece. This time, she sets out to travel 6,400 miles making use of the Trans-Siberian Railway, which is considered the longest railway line in the world. Joanna's journey begins in Hong Kong, where she spent some of her childhood. She takes a bullet train to Beijing, where she meets a woman who claims to have known the last Chinese emperor's favourite concubine, and officially joins the Trans-Siberian track. From there, she travels to the Great Wall, and takes an overnight train into Mongolia via the Gobi desert(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when a man collapses and dies outside a pub after a fight with his brother-in-law. The discovery of suspicious burn marks on the victim's forehead reveals sinister secrets and when a second body is found, the detectives are drawn into a world of superstition and family tensions. Murder mystery with John Nettles, John Hopkins and guest starring Geoffrey Whitehead(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet reopens the investigation into the murder of childhood friend Veronica, Rachel receives news that makes her personal life even more complicated, and a teenage murderer is successfully charged, but horrific events start to unravel following his arrest. Crime drama, guest starring Sally Lindsay, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones(n)

programme.title: The Halcyon
programme.desc: Emma is in charge of the hotel and she is put to the test hosting a society wedding for Lady Hamilton's friends the Ashworths, and has to stand up to the owner when there is an air-raid. Celina Radimsky comes to the hotel to meet Freddie, who tries to offer consolation after her husband's death, and Garland goes to the East End to help Peggy with some housekeeping, but has an encounter with a figure from his past while waiting outside a shop. Olivia Williams and Steven Mackintosh star, with a guest appearance by Jamie Cullum(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre refuses to believe Samir was violent at the airport and worries about him. Alma urges Ken to get on with his life without Denise(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike tells Deirdre to fight for Samir and offers her money to help. Raquel is touched when Sarah Louise asks to be a bridesmaid. Maud feels guilty about letting Maureen cope alone(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim tells Frank that she does not want to go away on honeymoon. Angharad asks Bernard to consider Jessica's feelings about Biff. Nick is nervous about his blind date(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda tells Zoe she is pleased to have her job back. Luke informs Angharad and Bernard that he is not leaving Emmerdale. Seth's pride gets in the way of reconciling with Betty(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When a guest at the Aidensfield Arms explains she's searching for her missing son, Blaketon offers to help - but as his hunt progresses, it becomes clear somebody is trying to do him harm. Peggy turns down David's offer of dinner on her birthday in favour of a date with an old flame, and Bellamy finally opens up about his troubles under pressure from Walker(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Roger returns to Liverpool, while Pop discovers Edith's new boyfriend George is a con-artist defrauding the ministry - and resolves to teach him a lesson. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida gets more than she bargained for during a visit to the fish and chip shop(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A face from the dead returns as Bernie prepares to receive his inherited fortune. Meanwhile, a girl brings shame on her father when she unexpectedly gives birth, and the residents of Aidensfield anxiously wait for news of Phil Bellamy(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris sees Zoe and Emma kissing. Angharad is shocked when Jessica runs away from home. Nick goes on another blind date. Vic is appalled when Viv pours all his alcohol away(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernard and Angharad continue their search for Jessica. Emma embarrasses Zoe by giving her a Valentine's present. Jan nags Dave after he loses his job(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel bores the staff with her wedding plans, and Deirdre rows with Samir. Vicky returns home, and Steve tries to get out of spending Christmas Day with his parents(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Samir announces he is returning to Morocco, and he wants Deirdre to go with him. Alf spots Martin kissing a student nurse. Curly gives Raquel a stellar Christmas present(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A face from the dead returns as Bernie prepares to receive his inherited fortune. Meanwhile, a girl brings shame on her father when she unexpectedly gives birth, and the residents of Aidensfield anxiously wait for news of Phil Bellamy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans returns as the young Inspector Morse in the first of four feature-length episodes. A baffling suicide, an anguished father searching for his missing daughter and the smash-and-grab robbery of medieval artefacts throw the detective in at the deep end as he resumes his role with Oxford City Police following a four-month absence from duty. As the locals go to the polls in a closely fought by-election and a beauty contest builds to its conclusion, Endeavour must navigate the choppy waters of both worlds as his investigation shakes the highest pillars of Oxford society. Co-starring Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, James Bradshaw, Jack Laskey and Sean Rigby(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a retired university fellow leads to a complex investigation for Morse and Lewis as they piece together a story of student suicide, drugs and the mysterious dismissal of a loyal college scout. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Phyllis Logan, Gabrielle Lloyd, Amanda Ryan and Tony Haygarth(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. A seat becomes vacant on the local council when Sam Popham is taken ill, so Pop stands in the election, and Mariette returns from holiday with Charley. David Jason stars(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is shocked when farmer Simon Langley-Smythe shoots her aunt's dog Snowy while out on a walk, and PC Younger fails to chase down children who have released livestock from the fields. Meanwhile, Carol is concerned when she sees Julie Langley-Smythe nursing a black eye(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles stock up on booze for their bar. The McAllisters prepare to leave the village. Seth and Vic set up their whisky still. Emma asks Zoe to move in with her(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the grand re-opening of the Woolpack. Frank and Kim arrive home from their honeymoon. Alan introduces his special guest for the re-opening - Ian Botham(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Martin feels guilty over his infidelity, Liz warns Deirdre about going to Morocco, Vicky hears Steve and Fiona are living together, and Mike wants to buy Deirdre's house(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack worries that Cliff will die before he has made his will. Ken is stunned to hear that Deirdre is emigrating. Tracy attacks Samir, accusing him of ruining her life(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is shocked when farmer Simon Langley-Smythe shoots her aunt's dog Snowy while out on a walk, and PC Younger fails to chase down children who have released livestock from the fields. Meanwhile, Carol is concerned when she sees Julie Langley-Smythe nursing a black eye(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Part one of two. The badly burned body of a disabled man turns up in a remote part of Manchester and a few days later another corpse is discovered. The detectives start to realise the victims were not just murdered, but tortured too. They make an arrest, but it becomes clear their investigation has just scratched the surface. Meanwhile, Janet throws her husband out and Rachel's estranged brother turns up on her doorstep. With Lisa Riley and Pippa Haywood(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: As Britain has grown into a nation of coffee lovers, Starbucks and Nespresso have become two of the world's biggest coffee brands. They have revolutionised what people drink and how they consume it. On the back of their success, both firms make bold claims about how their beans are ethically sourced and, in particular, that child labour is not used in their supply chains. But in this investigation, Dispatches travelled to Guatemala and discovered young children working long hours in gruelling conditions to pick coffee beans that supply these two coffee giants. Reporter Antony Barnett hears how a day's work can earn the children little more than the price of a Starbucks latte or a pack of Nespresso pods(n)

programme.title: M&S vs Waitrose: Which Is Better Value?
programme.desc: Documentary examining the ongoing rivalry between the supermarkets. Marks & Spencer has always had a special place in the nation's heart, with almost 1,000 stores and a reputation for quality stretching back 135 years. But Waitrose has proved a fierce rival. However, both stores face tough challenges in the future, not least from online retailers. Which of the two is best equipped to weather the high-street storm?(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: Make a New Life in the Country

Despite having no experience of the beer industry, they have sunk all their life savings into the project. Ben helps them with the day-to-day routine of cleaning barrels and making hops, as well as joining their research into developing a new recipe(n)

programme.title: My New Greek Life

Charley has her first day working alone in the hotel and Kate has to make a big decision(n)

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives

Ruth also meets designer Jody Bell at the luxurious Wellesley Hotel in London, to discuss her quest to become a high-street name in the fashion world(n)

Period drama, starring Jodie Foster, Chow Yun-Fat, Ling Bai, Syed Alwi, Tom Felton and Randall Duk Kim(n)

Drama, starring Russell Crowe, Jennifer Connelly, Ed Harris, Judd Hirsch, Paul Bettany and Christopher Plummer(n)

What starts as a break at a fun-filled trip to a luxury resort turns into something far more chaotic. Comedy, starring Will Ferrell, Mark Wahlberg, Mel Gibson and John Lithgow(n)

Drama, with Naomi Watts, Ewan McGregor and Tom Holland(n)

Father-of-two Tony, 59, is rushed in by helicopter medics following a head-on traffic collision, 85-year-old Eric arrives with ongoing stomach problems and 79-year-old Jim, who is experiencing breathing difficulties, quickly strikes up a rapport with the female staff and muses on what it means to be a gentleman(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Wiltshire's emergency services deal with people who live alone, including an elderly woman who has suffered a fall but is clearly longing for a chat. Police are also called out to a residential estate in Swindon where neighbours have reported consistent antisocial behaviour from Tracey, an aggressive and combustible woman who lives alone. She is brought in on a drunk and disorderly charge, but once in custody the officers see another side to her personality(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, the heli-medics are tasked with treating a man hit by a train, and cameras also follow cyclist Trevor, who suffers serious injuries after a collision with a car(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics treating a woman with brittle bone disease end up with another patient on their hands. A man collapses at the side of the road, but refuses to go to hospital(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsey heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The Craggy Island clerics face the prospect of an untimely death during the return flight from a visit to a holy shrine. Jack quickly commandeers the only parachute and bails out, taking the drinks trolley with him - leaving Ted and Dougal to their fate(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp demonstrates how old, forgotten furniture from a landfill site can be transformed, as she teams up with furniture-maker Simon Bell to revive a dining-room chair using clever painting tricks(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sun-worshippers Carol and Junior Smith have cashed in their savings and cannot wait to buy a holiday home where they can spend more quality time as a family. With a budget of £70,000, Jonnie Irwin shows them five properties near the popular Mar Menor in the south-east of Spain. Will they go for a bungalow with a rooftop terrace or a spacious townhouse that has them lost for words?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas shows engaged couple Ray and Sue five properties on Florida's gulf coast for their budget of £185,000(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A motorcyclist in Co Durham needs specialist attention after an accident, while medics are diverted from a training mission to a car crash on a country road(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A 93-year-old war veteran may have a life-threatening infection, and a four-year-old who is pale and vomiting has to be urgently seen by a specialist(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Belinda travels to Dick and Angel's chateau so that Angel can teach her the art of decoupage on a classic 1950s family dressing table. It's changeover day at Krys and Tim's chateau, as they sweat it out in 42-degree heat to get their 20-room chateau ready for their next booking. Budding chateau owners Elaine and David visit a 19th-century chateau with 18 rooms and two gîtes in Normandy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Maggie and Ian Dickson, who run Glenferndale B&B near Edinburgh, hope to be in with a chance of winning the title of best establishment as another round of the competition gets under way. However, two guests feel things are too homely, a spelling mistake on the breakfast menu is spotted, and the mood is not helped when the hosts have a bust-up(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Kristin Grantham hosts at Pitlessie House near the historic town of St Andrews in Fife, where the owner's slipper policy raises some eyebrows and two guests are alarmed by a stained mattress in their room. The afternoon's bagpiping competition is a hit, but cultural differences flare up over dinner and the proprietor's continental breakfast does not achieve the score she hoped for(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother-and-daughter team Eileen and Jannika Hopeakoski invite guests to their B&B Sea-La-Vie in Southsea, Portsmouth, for the third visit of the week, and while the guests think the stylish rooms are a fine vintage, host Eileen gets hot under the collar after discovering Ian and Maggie have been trying to strip back the mattress cover(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Craig and Marie Harris welcome their guests to the Peaks Inn in Derbyshire, but a few of the guests are far from impressed upon arrival. When the hosts reveal ferret racing is their chosen activity, a joke causes Eileen to lose her temper, so Ian and Maggie decide to stage a silent protest(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hoteliers meet one last time for feedback, during which they will discover how much their rivals thought their service was worth, and which of them was the most successful. Maggie and Ian Dickson decide to tackle complaints from Eileen and Jannika Hopeakoski head-on, while Craig and Marie Harris prepare for a fight after telling German competitor Kristin Grantham her venue and welcome lacked some much-needed warmth(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Tortoises Burt and Terrance become rivals in love when they pursue a breeding female. Meanwhile, six-year-old okapi K'Tusha nears the end of her first pregnancy, and keepers hope that her baby will help to conserve this highly-endangered species, and Asian elephant bull Aung-Bo senses that the three-day window for mating is about to begin(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Car enthusiast and Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend visit Plymouth to rescue a 25-year-old example of the world's bestselling two-seater sports car - the Mazda MX5. The particular motor belongs to former ambulance driver Paul and has been off the road for some time. With a '90s Japanese sports car in the workshop, Tim cannot let the chance to try out a few modifications go by(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Belinda and Lee welcome their first guests. Angela and Steve invite eight friends over to test-run their holiday business and Dick and Angel invite Tim and Krys over to their place(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs: The Street
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows two more self-build plots in Oxfordshire. Engineers Sean and Dianna are building an 'upside-down' house, with bedrooms downstairs so they can enjoy the views from their open-plan first floor. But an unexpected offer to work abroad upsets their carefully laid plans. Meanwhile, Garrie is building the largest house on the street, packed with expensive technology for wife Sue, who is disabled and has mental health issues(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend restore a 1972 Toyota Celica, one of the first Japanese sports coupes to find success on the global market, but spares are almost impossible to find(n)

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the car company's plant in northwest England, as work commences on a new range of 4x4s. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures

programme.desc: Malcolm, who has had a cardiac arrest, is put under emergency anaesthetic on the hospital helipad. Helicopter medics rush to help elderly driver Bill after his car flips in Whitley Bay. In Oxfordshire, a sidecar race comes to an abrupt end for brothers Paul and Shane(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: The crews are stretched to their limits as they deal with a 19-year-old man who has suffered a motorbike crash, and an elderly woman showing signs of a stroke but refusing treatment as it means she will leave behind her beloved husband of over 40 years(n)

programme.desc: Belinda and Lee welcome their first guests. Angela and Steve invite eight friends over to test-run their holiday business and Dick and Angel invite Tim and Krys over to their place(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Artist Residence in Penzance, Cornwall, where proprietors Justin and Charlie hope to impress their rivals with a pottery class. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

Can they find a solution or will their dreams turn to dust?(n)

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Home: Building for a Billionaire
programme.desc: Following the complex build of an extraordinary home costing millions in Mayfair, London, which belongs to self-made billionaire and philanthropist John Caudwell. Having sold Phones 4u in 2006 for just under 1.5 billion pounds, John is now aiming to create one of the most lavish and expensive homes ever to be renovated in Britain. programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John(n)

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A look at Wernher von Braun, the gifted young scientist whose ambition led him to abandon any sense of morality in pursuit of his dream of space flight(n)

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi

programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on an expedition along Dorset's Jurassic coast to explore the county's hidden Second World War story. Starting beside the defences on Chesil Beach, he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay. On his five-day walk he encounters stunning scenery and uncovers wartime acts of ingenuity and bravery. He also discovers the crucial part a swannery played in developing a secret weapon and reveals the role of Dorset in protecting Britain from invasion during the conflict(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris attempts to cross the length of Cuba, the only island in the Caribbean to boast an extensive railway network, starting in Havana in the west. This proves to be a major challenge given the unreliability of the service and the old trains regularly used, but makes for a fascinating journey to Chris's ultimate goal, the American naval base at Guantanamo Bay. Along the way he soaks up the sights and sounds of Havana, retraces the footsteps of the country's national hero Che Guevara, and visits the ghost town of Camilo Cienfuegos - a relic from Cuba's colonial sugar plantation era(n)

programme.title: The British Middle Class
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Tristram Hunt examines the social class's origins. He discovers how the backlash against the landed aristocracy began with a group of radical thinkers who craved progress and used their money from industry to begin a process of urban enlightenment. However, the growth of mass production led the newly moneyed leaders to move further away from the factories, beginning the trend for suburban living as a mark of status - though it meant the middle classes turning their back on where they came from(n)

programme.title: Benefits Britain: Life on the Dole
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the ups and downs of life for people on benefits in the UK, beginning with a visit to Liverpool to look at the effect of the bedroom tax. Lorna was living in a three-bedroom housing association home with her children, but when they moved out she was hit with a weekly £40 bedroom tax, which she has to pay out of her £72.40 employment and support allowance. Keith and Marie had been in their family home for 35 years, but after refusing to pay an extra £24 they were rehoused in a one-bedroom flat and forbidden from taking any of their animals with them(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The doctor splits the 10 remaining candidates for his medical team into two groups to treat a disabled man suffering from fainting spells, with the first to solve the problem being kept in the running to join his staff. When the patient begins to experience further symptoms, House is required to put the games to one side and take over the case himself. Hugh Laurie stars as the irascible medic(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord seeks urgent advice from eviction specialist Chris after finding his three-bedroom terrace has been stripped bare of the fittings, the flooring, and even the grass on the lawn. Landlady Rose gets a shock when she finally gains access to her property, and Dave from Peterborough Council's housing team comes across some of the worst damp and mould he has ever seen. Plus, Selina and Lee must return from America for a court case to recover £10,000 rent arrears on their UK property(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Kate faces trouble from unruly tenants, who became anti-social, and are now trying to sue her for serving them an eviction notice. Dhiren relates how the existing tenant of a house he bought stopped paying rent as soon as he took ownership, and Tim explains how a health scare left him seeking to sell his property - but a standing occupant refused to leave(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Steve have an emotional confrontation in Berkshire when a tearful woman accuses her landlord of fleecing her for extra rent, while Brian and Del travel to Luton to recover £4,500 owed by an elusive debtor who has been giving them the runaround(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. Meanwhile, in east London, two agents are confronted with a tenant who claims to have had no prior warning that he is being evicted from his tiny bedsit. Del and Brian try to recover £6,000 owed by a London hotel to a catering supply company, and Paul and Steve must tackle a difficult eviction(n)

programme.title: House

programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost £8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect £3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. programme.title: Columbo: Suitable for Framing (1971)
programme.desc: A millionaire art collector is thought to have been killed as the result of a random burglary. However, Columbo is convinced the dead man's nephew committed the crime to avoid being written out of the will, and embarks on a mission to ensnare his suspect. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Ross Martin and Don Ameche(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Crucial Game (1972)
programme.desc: The owner of an American football team is found dead in a swimming pool, and his drowning is regarded as an accident. The down-at-heel detective is convinced that foul play was involved and sets out to prove his case - but his chief suspect has a cast-iron alibi that places him miles away from the scene of the crime. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk and Robert Culp(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Playback (1975)
programme.desc: An inventor tries to stop his mother-in-law from exposing his infidelities by murdering her in front of a security camera - and tampering with the recording to make it look like the killing occurred at a time when he had an alibi. However, Columbo sees through his hi-tech ruse and tries to expose the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Oskar Werner and Gena Rowlands(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Greenhouse Jungle (1972)
programme.desc: A penniless rare-plant collector, desperate for funds to support his expensive hobby, fakes his nephew's kidnapping to get his hands on a trust fund. However, the supposed victim turns up dead, prompting Columbo to take action to unmask the killer. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Bradford Dillman and Ray Milland(n)

programme.title: Columbo Goes to College (1991)
programme.desc: Two criminology students murder their professor and cover their tracks with false clues. They think they are on the way to outwitting everyone - but find themselves at the bottom of the class when Columbo intervenes. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Stephen Caffrey, Gary Hershberger and Robert Culp(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Most Dangerous Match (1973)
programme.desc: An American chess champion fears he is about to be beaten by a superior player from Russia, and decides the only sure-fire way he can avoid defeat is by murdering his opponent. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Laurence Harvey and Jack Kruschen(n)

programme.title: Columbo: The Conspirators (1978)
programme.desc: A terrorist posing as an Irish author heads a peace organization while secretly running guns for the IRA in Belfast. When he kills an arms dealer who tried to swindle him, the writer is sure he has covered his tracks - until Columbo shambles on to the scene. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Clive Revill, Jeanette Nolan and Bernard Behrens(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan and Wheeler are partnered with reluctant detective Harry Williams from the gang unit to investigate the murder of a local rapper who was encouraging his fans to report crimes. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Amaro declines an invite to his father's wedding, but he is forced to get involved in his dad's life after a fight breaks out at the ceremony. Danny Pino stars(n)

programme.desc: A rape victim is found in the street - groggy after being drugged - and in her confused state she mistakenly accuses the wrong man of attacking her, with alarming consequences. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The drama about an NYPD team specialising in sexual crimes returns. Benson tries to juggle work and her new role as a foster mother to Noah Porter. Meanwhile, a new detective assigned to the unit gets off to a rocky start. Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek star(n)

programme.desc: After a woman is found dead by a neighbour, a conspiracy unfolds when detectives discover her boyfriend has also been murdered - with his body placed beneath his house. programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A woman confesses to murdering her husband, but the evidence clears her of any wrongdoing and raises several disturbing new lines of inquiry - including the possibility that a serial killer may be stalking the city. Elsewhere, Hawkes forces Danny to concentrate on his recovery, and Flack's behaviour is called into question. Drama, starring Gary Sinise(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The team tries to help a seriously injured and traumatised woman remember the events that led to the fatal shooting of her street dancer boyfriend in their hotel room. Gary Sinise stars(n)

programme.title: The Mysteries of Laura
programme.desc: The return of the comedy drama about a New York homicide detective Laura Diamond as she navigates juggling a difficult work and home life. When ex-husband Jake returns to work a week early to help with the case, it looks as though Laura is stuck with her new boss for good(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Callers to the emergency services report seeing a photograph of a murdered woman on the internet, and when Green and Cassady investigate, they realise her clothes do not fit properly. They trace her unusual attire to a mentally disturbed man, who protests that the victim's daughter asked him to kill her mother. Crime drama, starring Jesse L Martin and Milena Govich(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An eight-year-old girl is killed in a building explosion, triggering an investigation that leads Green and Cassady to the property owner's ex-wife. Although they have no firm proof, McCoy pursues her in the courts - but Rubirosa finds a vital clue that leads the case in another direction(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A shooting occurs at City Hall leaving a councilman dead and a water inspector injured. Once a suspect is identified, the prosecution has a hard time securing the murder weapon as evidence, as it transpires it was obtained by the FBI in a covert operation. Jerry Orbach stars(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A Gulf War veteran is arrested on suspicion of killing a peace campaigner whose views he opposed because he believed they were anti-American - and cites the recent death of his son in Afghanistan as the cause of his extreme emotional disturbance. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green and Cassady investigate an arson attack on a church - but the discovery of a man's body among the charred ruins, obviously killed before the blaze was started, turns the case into a murder inquiry. The victim later proves to have been a school tutor - who may have been murdered for teaching the theory of evolution(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green and Cassady investigate the fatal shooting of female rapper Clarice James. The clues initially lead them to a shady music producer, but suspicion later falls on a jeweller who claims the victim owed him a large sum of money. Police drama, starring Milena Govich and Jesse L Martin(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Jerry Orbach's final appearance in the crime drama. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An Iraqi immigrant kills a female veteran of the second Gulf War for torturing her brother at Abu Ghraib prison. However, the detectives learn the accused's lawyer plans to defend his client as an enemy combatant, immune from prosecution under the terms of the Geneva Convention, and that they may have no jurisdiction over the case. Starring Jesse L Martin and Dennis Farina(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The members of an evangelical church close ranks to protect their leader, an influential preacher whose wife has been found dead. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: When the assistant of a female videogame developer is attacked at a convention the team goes undercover online and discovers a world of misogynistic abuse by gamers. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team tries to stop a sex-trafficking ring after a mother goes undercover to find her daughter, who has been taken by the gang, and later Benson uncovers a shocking truth. Hard-hitting and emotional drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera

Nick visits Stafford to meet seasoned CCTV operator and ex-police officer Barbara, who helps catch a drug user after spotting him on camera. programme.desc: A man falls from a bridge in what appears to be a straightforward suicide, but lab results reveal he was shot in the chest before being thrown off. Crime drama, starring Hill Harper(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and the team uncover the lair of serial murderer the 'Compass Killer' while investigating how he escaped from the World's Fair site in Queens. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise(n)

programme.title: The Mysteries of Laura

However, as several details do not make sense, Laura is not convinced and badgers Captain Santiani into taking the case further. Starring Debra Messing and Josh Lucas(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Green and Cassady look into the murder of Carl Mullaly, who had recently been profiled on HardFocus, a magazine programme that identifies sex offenders. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The CEO of a military equipment contractor is murdered, leading Cassady and Green to initially suspect anti-war protesters. The victim's widow later receives a Purple Heart medal, linking the act to the killing of a soldier in Iraq, which prompts the detectives to track down the dead serviceman's former comrades. Guest starring Titus Welliver (The Good Wife), with Milena Govich(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy vows to find those responsible when a key witness to the killing of a drug dealer is shot dead. Suspicion falls on the accused's cousin, a local hoodlum with a number of charges to his name, and another associate who refuses to make a deal with the investigators. Sam Waterson, Elisabeth Rohm and Jerry Orbach star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A cross-dresser is suspected of killing and dismembering a cantankerous former boxer - but the team is unable to find vital body parts, making it impossible for the medical examiner to prove the case is murder. Crime drama, starring Jerry Orbach(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant dies of ricin poisoning and the detectives' inquiries link him and his brother to a prostitution ring trafficking young women into New York. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the abduction and murder of a child, following a lead to a suspected serial paedophile, who is found holding another boy captive. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is accused of murdering her husband's mistress. Although the crime is found to be premeditated, the defendant's lawyer claims his client's actions were prompted by post-traumatic stress syndrome arising from the September 11 terrorist attack. Starring Dennis Farina(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A recently paroled prison gang member is shot dead, and suspicion falls on former crime associates - until his battle behind bars with a corrections officer comes to light. programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series focuses on Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

But storm clouds are gathering in Europe, and Lady Persie falls under the spell of Oswald Mosley and his British Union of Fascists, forming an alliance that will drag her into moral and physical peril. programme.desc: A group of courageous Italian nuns risks everything during the Second World War. Drama, starring Lilli Palmer, Sylvia Syms, Yvonne Mitchell, Ronald Lewis and Albert Lieven(n)

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow heads for 18th-century Paris to discover how the insanitary living conditions endured by its poorest residents helped bring about the French Revolution. He visits a perfumer to re-create some of the city's unsavoury smells, and finds out what happened to the thousands of bodies that overflowed from cemeteries during the Terror of 1793. He also tries his hand at tanning leather the traditional way - using dog faeces and urine(n)

programme.title: Spitfire Women
programme.desc: The story of the 168 female pilots who served in Britain's Air Transport Auxiliary - an organisation responsible for delivering aircraft to the RAF on the front lines of battle during the Second World War. Despite having no radio or navigation equipment, ATA pilots were expected to fly any kind of aircraft to the destinations where they were most needed, often risking their lives in the process. The film also features interviews with the last surviving female veterans, who speak about the dangers they encountered in the skies and the discrimination they faced back home(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
